title: Fair Distribution of Cookies in a Bakery

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
An integer array A of size n representing the number of cookies each child has initially
and an integer array B of size m representing the number of cookies each child can take in a visit
The input will be space-separated integers. A and B will be on separate lines.
statement.outputFormat: A binary string with n letters, where '1' represents a child who is happy and '0' otherwise.
statement.notes:
You should return the least greedy way of distributing the cookies so that all children are happy.
Each child can only visit a child once.

hints.text: Find the largest index where B[k] > A[k]

editorial.title: Fair Distribution of Cookies in a Bakery
editorial.intuition: Try to make the first child's cookies as high as possible by distributing cookies to other children if necessary.

functionSignature.cpp: bool solve(int *A, int *B, int n, int m);
functionSignature.java: public boolean solve(int[] A, int[] B, int n, int m);
